MEETING NOTES — NGIS PHASE 3 STEERING COMMITTEE
Date: March 8, 2026 | Attendees: Director Okafor, Alice Tran, Bob Morrison, ADM Clarke

Item 5 (raised by ADM Clarke):

Following the recent cyber incident affecting a peer department's primary
data centre, ADM Clarke raised the requirement to add a dedicated disaster
recovery (DR) site to the NGIS Phase 3 scope.

ADM Clarke's request:
"Given the current threat environment and Treasury Board's updated guidance
on GC infrastructure resilience, I would like the project team to
investigate adding a new disaster recovery site as part of the Phase 3
consolidation. This would involve identifying an additional colocation
facility — potentially Borden or Miramichi — and ensuring that all
workloads migrated during Phase 3 have a hot-standby replica at the DR
site. This is not currently in our SOW and I understand it represents a
material scope addition, but I believe it is essential for business
continuity compliance."

Project Manager Response (Alice Tran):
Alice acknowledged the request and noted that a new disaster recovery
site was not included in the current Statement of Work (SOW-NGIS-P3-2024-001).
She committed to conducting a preliminary scoping assessment and presenting
options to the steering committee by March 15. Any formal scope addition
would require a change request, ADM approval, and Treasury Board
notification if the cost impact exceeds 15% of the approved budget.

Action: Alice Tran to prepare DR site options analysis. Due: March 15.
